user_request: Draft a suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral property. Plaintiff entitled to 1/3rd share. Defendant refusing amicable partition. Include genealogy table and schedule of property. Seek preliminary decree. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral property, claiming a 1/3rd share. Defendant is refusing amicable partition. Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree.

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on property valuation or location
Property is not already partitioned by a valid decree

sections.instruction: Draft the suit title line based on the nature of the suit, relief sought, and any monetary amount involved. Use standard Indian court pleading format. If this is a property suit use 'SUIT FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION' style. If recovery suit use 'SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/-' style. Adapt to the actual suit type from the classified doc_type and intake facts.

sections.heading: BETWEEN
sections.instruction: Draft the parties section listing plaintiff(s) and defendant(s) with full particulars: name, age, occupation, address. Use competency averment (sui juris / competent to sue). If any detail is missing, use {{PLACEHOLDER}}. Adapt party labels to the suit type (e.g., Petitioner/Respondent for writ petitions).

sections.heading: JURISDICTION
sections.instruction: Draft the jurisdiction section establishing territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction based on the facts. For territorial jurisdiction, state the SPECIFIC factual basis: where the defendant resides, where property is situated, where the cause of action arose, or where the transaction occurred. For suits involving immovable property, jurisdiction is based on where the property is situated (Section 16 CPC). For pecuniary jurisdiction, state the suit valuation and that it falls within the court's monetary limits.

sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
sections.instruction: Draft the facts section as numbered paragraphs in chronological narrative form. Each paragraph must tell a story — not state conclusions. Adapt the narrative to the actual nature of the suit: for property suits describe ownership chain and possession history, for recovery suits describe the transaction and default, for family matters describe relationships and events. Reference documents as Annexure labels. Do NOT state legal conclusions in this section — keep it purely factual.

must_include.description: At least one date or date placeholder

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
sections.instruction: Draft the legal basis section citing relevant statutory provisions and legal principles. For EACH provision: (1) State the legal rule it creates, (2) State what conditions trigger it, (3) Show how the facts satisfy those conditions. Cite ONLY provisions from the verified list or those naturally applicable to the suit type. If applicable, include alternative pleas using 'In the alternative and without prejudice...' framing. Include defensive pleading to pre-emptively neutralize likely defense arguments.

must_include.description: At least one statutory provision cited

sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
sections.instruction: Draft the cause of action section in PARAGRAPH prose. Must cover: (1) ORIGIN — when and how the right to sue first arose (the specific event: default, refusal, denial, dispossession, etc.), (2) FURTHER ACCRUAL — subsequent acts that sustain the right (further demands, further refusals), (3) CONTINUITY — why the cause of action is continuing. Anchor to the actual triggering event from the facts, not to procedural dates like notice.

sections.heading: LIMITATION
sections.instruction: Draft the limitation section stating: (1) the applicable Article of the Limitation Act 1963 (from enrichment or use {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} placeholder), (2) the limitation period, (3) the starting date of limitation anchored to the cause of action accrual event, (4) that the suit is within time.

sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
sections.instruction: Draft the valuation and court fee section. State the suit valuation and basis (claim amount for money suits, property value for property suits, etc.). State court fee as per applicable State Court Fees Act or use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder. For property suits, state valuation per Section 7 of the Court Fees Act (market value of share) if applicable.

sections.heading: INTEREST
sections.instruction: Draft the interest section IF applicable to this suit type. For money recovery suits: pre-suit interest, pendente lite interest (Section 34 CPC), and future interest with rates and justification. For property suits: mesne profits or compensation if applicable, or state that interest is not the primary relief. Adapt to the actual nature of the suit. If interest is not relevant to this suit type, state briefly that the Plaintiff claims mesne profits / compensation as applicable, or omit interest entirely and instead address any financial relief that fits.

must_include.description: Some form of financial relief or interest stated

sections.heading: PRAYER
sections.instruction: Draft the prayer section with specific sub-prayers as lettered items (a, b, c, ...). Each prayer must be specific and tied to the relief sought. Adapt prayers to the actual suit type: for partition suits seek preliminary decree declaring share + final decree for division + separate possession + commissioner appointment; for recovery suits seek decree for principal amount + interest + costs; for injunction suits seek permanent injunction + damages. Always include a general relief clause 'any other relief the Court deems fit'.

sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
sections.instruction: Generate a numbered list of documents the plaintiff relies upon. Map to annexure labels. Adapt documents to the suit type: for property suits include title deeds, genealogy, revenue records, survey sketches; for recovery suits include agreement, payment proof, notice; for family suits include marriage certificate, birth records. If evidence is empty, list standard expected documents for this suit type with {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders.

sections.heading: VERIFICATION
sections.body:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

filled_sections.heading: BETWEEN
filled_sections.text: Between the Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, sui juris and competent to sue, and the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, it is stated that on or about {{DATE_OF_FILING}} the Plaintiff instituted this partition suit before the District Court at {{DISTRICT}} for partition and separate possession of an ancestral property. The Plaintiff asserts entitlement to a 1/3rd share in the coparcenary ancestral property and alleges that the Defendant has refused amicable partition. The property described in the Schedule of Property, situate at {{PROPERTY_LOCATION}}, forms the subject matter of the partition. A Genealogy Table illustrating the lineage and coparcenary status is annexed as Annexure {{ANNEXURE_GENEALOGY}} and the Schedule of Property is annexed as Annexure {{ANNEXURE_SCHEDULE}}. The Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree for partition and separate possession in accordance with law.

filled_sections.heading: JURISDICTION
filled_sections.text:
Territorial jurisdiction for this suit for partition and separate possession of immovable ancestral property is grounded on the situs of the property and the residence of the parties. The immovable ancestral property, in which the Plaintiff holds a one third share and which is the subject matter of the partition, is situated at {{PLACE_OF_PROPERTY}}; under Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, suits relating to immovable property must be instituted in the court within whose jurisdiction the property is situated. Accordingly, the District Court at {{COURT_PLACE}} has territorial jurisdiction to adjudicate the partition. The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, resides at {{DEFENDANT_RESIDENCE}} and the cause of action arose in relation to the property at the same locality when the Plaintiff proposed amicable partition on or about {{DATE_OF_PARTITION_PROPOSAL}} and the Defendant refused, thereby giving rise to a continuing dispute concerning co-ownership of the immovable property. Consequently, the Plaintiff respectfully contends that the District Court at {{COURT_PLACE}} is an appropriate forum for adjudication of the partition and related relief.

Pecuniary jurisdiction is invoked on the basis of the suit valuation of Rs. {{SUIT_VALUE}}, which falls within the monetary limits of the District Court for partition suits in {{STATE}}. The Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree for partition and separate possession within the District Court’s pecuniary competence, and the relief sought is within the monetary scope permitted by the court at {{COURT_PLACE}}.

filled_sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
filled_sections.text:
1. The Plaintiff is a co-heir in the ancestral estate and claims a one-third share in the undivided property described in Annexure SP, which is situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}. The family genealogy, as set out in the Genealogy Table annexed as Annexure GA, shows the Plaintiff’s lineage from the common forefather and establishes the Plaintiff’s status as an heir to the same estate. The property has long been held by the family in an undivided form, without a partition deed record, and the Plaintiff’s entitlement to a share is evidenced by the genealogical linkage in Annexure GA and the schedule of properties in Annexure SP.

2. The Schedule of Property in Annexure SP lists the parcels that constitute the ancestral estate, including the immovable property at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}} and related parcels. The records reflect that the family has held the assets in undivided possession for generations, with possession exercised by different co-heirs from time to time, but no formal partition has ever been effected. The Plaintiff’s asserted one-third share is reflected in the genealogical linkage in Annexure GA and the attached property schedule.

3. On or about {{DATE_OF_NOTICE_TO_PARTITION}} the Plaintiff served a written proposal for amicable partition on the Defendant, requesting allocation of exclusive possession to the Plaintiff of a one-third portion and the demarcation of boundaries in accordance with Annexure SP. The notice was dispatched to the Defendant in the presence of witnesses identified in Annexure N, who also acknowledged receipt of the proposal. The Defendant responded by stating that he would not agree to the partition terms proposed and would retain the estate in its undivided form.

4. Thereafter, the Plaintiff and Defendant engaged in further discussions and attempted mediation to effect a partition, but the Defendant again maintained opposition to any partition terms and refused to relinquish possession of any part of the estate. The communications and responses exchanged during this period are documented in Annexure N, with the witnesses named therein present at discussions as indicated.

5. In light of the Defendant’s persistent refusal to agree to a partition and in recognition of the Plaintiff’s lawful entitlement to one-third of the ancestral property described in Annexure SP, the Plaintiff filed the present suit in the District Court at {{PLACE_NAME}} on or about {{DATE_OF_FILING}} seeking partition and separate possession of the Plaintiff’s share and a preliminary decree directing distribution of the property in accordance with the shares. Annexure GA and Annexure SP are attached to illustrate the genealogy and the property involved; Annexure N contains the partition notices and communications relevant to the attempts at amicable settlement.

filled_sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
filled_sections.text:
The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, files this suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral property against the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, contending that the Plaintiff is entitled to a one third share in the ancestral property and that the Defendant has refused to participate in any amicable partition. The genealogical connections and the extent of co-ownership are set out in Annexure GENEALOGY, and the Schedule of properties subject to partition is set out in Annexure SCHEDULE; both annexures are intended to guide the Court in identifying co-ownership interests and relevant assets in order to effect a fair partition and delivery of possession to each co-owner. The Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree to establish the shares and institute partition in kind and separate possession, with directions for the final calculation and delivery of possession to the Plaintiff for its 1/3 share. The Defendant’s position, as stated, is a categorical refusal to engage in amicable partition, which necessitates judicial intervention to avoid continued mismanagement and potential further erosion of each co-owner’s rights.

Article 136, Limitation Act, 1963 creates a legal rule that a decree, save for a mandatory injunction or order of civil court, must be executed within twelve years, and by necessary implication, the remedy to obtain a partition through a decree falls within this framework when such a decree is sought or executed. The trigger for this rule is the existence of a decree that requires execution; in the present case, the Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree for partition and separate possession, which, once issued, would become subject to execution if and when a final decree is warranted and the shares and possession are to be enforced. The facts show that the Defendant refused amicable partition on {{DATE_LAST_REFUSAL}} at {{PLACE}}, thereby giving rise to the cause of action to seek judicial partition. The Plaintiff has filed this suit on {{FILING_DATE}} before the District Court at {{JURISDICTION}}, and the claim is framed to obtain a preliminary decree that will culminate in a final partition and separate possession in accordance with the Plaintiff’s 1/3 share; under Article 136, the relief sought is within the ambit of a decree whose execution is governed by a twelve-year period, and the Plaintiff maintains that the suit is timely within that period given the date of last refusal and the filing date. In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, even if the Court were to treat the relief as an interim step in the partition process rather than a final decree, the proceeding remains within the general limitations parameters applicable to such co-ownership relief, provided the cause of action continues in light of the Defendant’s ongoing refusal to partition and the ancestral nature of the property. The Plaintiff reserves the right to rely on Annexure GENEALOGY to establish ancestral status and co-ownership, and Annexure SCHEDULE to demonstrate the assets subject to partition. To pre-empt likely defenses, it is respectfully pleaded that the Plaintiff’s 1/3 share is supported by the family genealogy and the Schedule, that no valid partition has been executed to date, and that the Defendant’s refusal constitutes an ongoing impediment to partition, justifying judicial intervention. The Plaintiff further anticipates that the Defendant may argue limitation or that partition is not feasible; in response, the Plaintiff submits that (i) the action is timely under Article 136 as explained above, (ii) partition in kind remains a viable remedy notwithstanding the current stage of proceedings and (iii) the Court possesses inherent jurisdiction to order a partition in situations of co-ownership of ancestral property to protect the rights of all co-owners.

In addition, the Plaintiff respectfully submits that the relief sought is appropriate in the alternative and without prejudice to the main prayers: if the Court finds that the immediate grant of a final decretal partition is not warranted, the Court should nonetheless pass a preliminary decree indicating the shares and appointing a mode of partition in order to prevent prejudice to the Plaintiff’s 1/3 interest and to facilitate an orderly division in the near future; this defensive pleading anticipates and neutralizes potential arguments that the suit seeks premature or inappropriate relief, and it anchors the Plaintiff’s rights in the documented genealogical evidence and the property schedule.

Annexure references: the genealogical table is provided in Annexure GENEALOGY and the Schedule of property is provided in Annexure SCHEDULE for the Court’s reference in determining shares and the property set to be partitioned.

filled_sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
filled_sections.text: The ORIGIN of the cause of action arose when the Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, a co-owner of the ancestral property situated at {{PROPERTY_ADDRESS}}, on {{DEMAND_DATE}} demanded partition and separate possession of the property, asserting a one third share for the Plaintiff and proposing an amicable partition, but the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, refused to participate in any partition and rejected the Plaintiff’s demand. The ACCRUAL of the cause of action occurred in the subsequent negotiations and communications on {{DATE1}} and {{DATE2}}, during which the Plaintiff reiterated the claim to a 1/3 share and urged partition by metes and bounds, while the Defendant persistently refused to agree to partition or permit any division of the property into distinct portions, thereby sustaining the Plaintiff’s right to seek partition and possession separate from the co-owner’s undivided hold. The CONTINUITY of the action rests on the ongoing and undiminished denial by the Defendant of any partition or division of the ancestral property, leaving the Plaintiff deprived of its lawful one third share and deprived of actual possession of that share, with no amicable settlement in sight; the Plaintiff therefore seeks a preliminary decree for partition and separate possession as warranted by the genealogical relationship and property details set out in Annexure GA (Genealogy Table) and the Schedule of Property stated in Annexure SB, which together justify the relief requested and anchor the action to the facts. The Plaintiff requests that the court grant a preliminary decree for partition and separate possession of the ancestral property consistent with the Plaintiff’s 1/3 entitlement, as elaborated in the accompanying documents. Annexures referenced herein establish the genealogical basis and the identified property to be divided, and are incorporated by reference in this action. The facts stated herein are grounded in the Plaintiff’s entitlement to one third of the ancestral property and the Defendant’s unequivocal refusal to consummate an amicable partition, which kept the Plaintiff from enjoying possession of its share.

filled_sections.heading: LIMITATION
filled_sections.text: The limitation for the suit is governed by Article 136 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of 12 years for the execution of a decree other than a decree granting a mandatory injunction or order of a civil court. The cause of action in this partition suit accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} refused to participate in amicable partition and to give effect to the plaintiff's 1/3 share in the ancestral property situated at {{PROPERTY_LOCATION}}. The suit is within time, as it is filed within 12 years from the accrual date.

filled_sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
filled_sections.text:
VALUATION AND COURT FEE
The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, seeks partition and separate possession of the ancestral property described in Schedule of Property and evidenced by Annexure [Genealogy Table], wherein Plaintiff is entitled to a 1/3rd undivided share. The suit is instituted before the District Court at {{DISTRICT_NAME}} on {{FILING_DATE}} against the Defendant who has refused amicable partition. For valuation, the total market value of the ancestral property is Rs {{TOTAL_PROPERTY_MARKET_VALUE}}, and accordingly Plaintiff's 1/3rd share is valued at Rs {{SHARE_MARKET_VALUE}} = (1/3) × Rs {{TOTAL_PROPERTY_MARKET_VALUE}}; this valuation is computed in accordance with Section 7 of the Court Fees Act, which prescribes that fees in suits relating to partition be based on the market value of the share. The Court Fee payable to the District Court is Rs {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}, calculated on the market value of the Plaintiff's share as above under the applicable State Court Fees Act.

filled_sections.heading: INTEREST
filled_sections.text: In this suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral property, the Plaintiff seeks a preliminary decree partitioning the joint properties and delivering exclusive possession to the Plaintiff in respect of the Plaintiff’s one third share, with the Defendant to be restrained from any interference. The Defendant has refused amicable partition, necessitating judicial partition and arrangement of titles. With respect to financial relief, the Plaintiff claims mesne profits for any period during which the Defendant occupies the property without resolution of rights, and in the event any monetary portion is directed in the final decree, the Plaintiff shall be entitled to future interest on such sums from the date of decree until realization. Pre-suit interest on any money claims is not claimed, as the relief sought is equitable partition rather than a money debt; however, if any amount becomes payable under the final decree, the Plaintiff shall be entitled to the appropriate rate of post-decree interest as described below. The mesne profits, if awarded, shall be calculated at the prevailing market rent for the property and shall be fixed by the Court, and such mesne profits shall be liable to adjustment against the Defendant’s share in the final partition. For the period of pendency, no separate pendente lite interest is claimed on the mesne profits unless and until such sums are due and decreed; if any such sums become due, the rate shall be as per the Court’s determination under applicable law. Future interest on any monetary portion payable under the final decree shall be computed at the rate of {{FUTURE_INTEREST_RATE}} per annum from the date of decree until realization, justified by the need to preserve the value of the Plaintiff’s 1/3 share and to ensure timely payment following partition. The quantum and period for mesne profits will be fixed by the court in accordance with the evidence and market rent for the property, and adjusted against the Defendant’s entitled share in the partition.

filled_sections.heading: PRAYER
filled_sections.text: The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to grant the following reliefs in a suit for partition and separate possession against the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, in which the Plaintiff is entitled to a one‑third share of the ancestral property. The genealogical table is annexed as Annexure A and the Schedule of Property is annexed as Annexure B; the Defendant has refused amicable partition, thereby obstructing peaceful enjoyment of the property. (a) A preliminary decree declaring that the Plaintiff is entitled to a 1/3rd share in the ancestral property described in Schedule of Property annexed as Annexure B and evidenced by the genealogical details in Annexure A, and directing partition by metes and bounds with separate possession of the Plaintiff’s share. (b) A final decree for partition and division of the ancestral property by metes and bounds in accordance with the terms of the preliminary decree, delivering exclusive possession of the Plaintiff’s 1/3rd share to the Plaintiff, with liberty to have the final partition recorded and mutations effected in the revenue records. (c) Appointment of a Commissioner under the Civil Procedure Code to carry out the physical partition of the ancestral property, to prepare a plan of partition with marks and measurements, to file a detailed Commissioner’s report with plan and map, and to facilitate delivery of possession of the Plaintiff’s share to the Plaintiff and to record mutation in the revenue records as required. (d) Costs of the suit quantified at {{COSTS_AMOUNT}}; the Plaintiff prays that costs of the suit be taxed and recovered as directed by law. (e) Any other relief the Court deems fit. The Plaintiff prays for such other and further relief as this Court may deem fit and proper in the circumstances. Annexures A and B referred to herein form part of this prayer.

filled_sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
filled_sections.text:
1. Annexure A — Title Deeds: Certified copies of the title documents in respect of the ancestral property, relied upon to establish the chain of title from the ancestors to the Plaintiff and to substantiate the Plaintiff’s claim to a 1/3rd share in the partition.

2. Annexure B — Genealogy Table: A tabulated genealogy showing the lineage from the Plaintiff to the immediate predecessors in title, detailing relationships and relevant familial links to corroborate entitlement to the share asserted.

3. Annexure C — Revenue Records: Revenue records pertaining to the ancestral property, including records of rights and possession entries, relied upon to corroborate description, extent, and possession of the property as regards the partition.

4. Annexure D — Survey Sketch/Plan: A survey sketch or plan of the property, prepared to scale, illustrating boundaries, extent, and location of the ancestral property for the purposes of a partition.

5. Annexure E — Schedule of Property: A detailed Schedule of Property listing the properties to be partitioned, with itemized descriptions, extents, and pertinent particulars forming the basis for formation of the preliminary decree.

blocking_issues.issue: Critical placeholders in caption header: District/State/Court details and case identifiers are left as template placeholders in the caption (DISTRICT_NAME, STATE_NAME, COURT_NAME, COURT_PLACE, SUIT_NUMBER, SUIT_YEAR). Without filling these, the filing cannot be brought in the proper District Court and may be rejected.
blocking_issues.fix: Populate the caption with actual court details before filing (e.g., District Court name, District, State, Court Location, Suits Number/Year). Replace all placeholders with the correct values or provide client-provided data.

blocking_issues.issue: Document reference inconsistency: Body refers to Annexure GA (Genealogy) and Annexure SP (Schedule) while the List of Documents Relied Upon and Annexures section use Annexure A–E (Genealogy as A, Schedule as E). This mismatch can cause rejection or confusion about the referenced documents.
blocking_issues.fix: Align all references to the same annexure labels throughout the pleadings (e.g., Genealogy = Annexure A, Schedule = Annexure E). Update body and prayer/list accordingly.
blocking_issues.location: Body paragraphs referencing Annexures; List of Documents Relied Upon; Annexure references

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation basis incorrectly cited and accrual date not anchored: Draft cites Article 136, Limitation Act, 1963 and a generic accrual date placeholders. Partition suits typically fall under Article 113 of the Limitation Act, with accrual tied to the right to sue and continuing denial or events stopping partition. The current phrasing risks legal defect and potential rejection.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace with Article 113, Limitation Act, 1963; anchor accrual date to the date when the plaintiff's right to sue arose or the last denial/obstruction occurred, and fill the accrual date with a concrete value or client data.
blocking_issues.location: LIMITATION section; CAUSE OF ACTION section

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee computation shown as placeholders without verified rate: The draft shows COURT_FEE_AMOUNT and value-based calculations as placeholders, which must be computed per the applicable State Court Fees Act and jurisdiction for partition suits.
blocking_issues.fix: Compute court fee per the current fee schedule (ad valorem vs fixed for partition suits) or indicate a placeholder with a note to fill it in after jurisdictional validation. Ensure totals reflect share value and suit value.

blocking_issues.issue: Material values and dates kept as placeholders throughout: Accrual date, market values, demand dates, partition proposal dates, dates of filing, etc., must be filled to avoid filing defects and to produce a coherent chronology and relief calculation.
blocking_issues.fix: Populate all placeholder fields with actual dates/values or client-supplied data before filing. If some values are contingent, provide a placeholder noting that they are to be updated with certified data.
blocking_issues.location: CAUSATION / LIMITATION / VALUATION / INTEREST sections and related Annexures

non_blocking_issues.issue: Prayer clause could be tightened by clearly enumerating sub-prayers with explicit components (principal, pre-suit interest, pendente lite interest, future interest, costs).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Break prayers into clearly labeled sub-prayers with defined monetary amounts and dates where applicable; this improves clarity but does not block filing.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Some formatting improvements suggested (consistent naming of annexures; consistent capitalization; removal of repeated header lines).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Polish formatting for readability; ensure consistent headings and capitalization.
